response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with genuine high-performance systems experience, but his career is centered on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), not C++. His only professional C++ work was roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and C++ does not appear in his current skills list. The role demands deep, hands-on C++/STL expertise plus industrial-domain knowledge (IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, message brokers in an automation context), none of which is evidenced. Despite impressive breadth and seniority, the core technical fit is weak.
strengths: Low-latency, concurrent systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) on services handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing advocacy and mentoring/workshops
Advantageous-skills coverage: Node.js, React, cloud deployment (AWS, Kubernetes), and some embedded exposure
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, a decade ago); C++ absent from stated skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or MQTT/OT-protocol background
Recent trajectory is full-stack web/product work, diverging from the networked multi-threaded C++ focus of this role
